text.title: Agent Kill-Switch Design Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example applying agent-kill-switch-design to a representative case. Walks step-by-step through the procedure, shows the produced artefact, and highlights the rules it satisfies.

section.title: Setup
section.p: Context: a team that matches the Applies-If conditions for agent-kill-switch-design. Inputs are populated per the Prerequisites table.

list.item: Step 1: Design 3-scope toggle: global, per-tenant, per-tool. — produced scope design.
Step 2: Implement toggle propagation; benchmark p95 latency. — produced ≤5s latency verified.
Step 3: Wire structured kill-event log to incident channel. — produced events live.
Step 4: Write runbook: who, what, when, and how to recover. — produced runbook.
Step 5: Test kill + recover in staging on day 0; schedule per-release retest. — produced tested.

section.title: Final artefact
section.p: The team commits the final artefact (per spec shape) at the version-controlled path declared in the output contract. Owner signs and links it to downstream consumers.

list.item: r1: Kill switch has 3 scopes: global (all tenants), per-tenant, per-tool. Each scope is independently togglable.
r2: End-to-end kill latency (toggle → no new requests reach LLM) is ≤5 seconds at p95.
r3: Kill switch is tested in production-equivalent staging on day 0 AND retested at every release.
r4: Recovery path is documented and tested: who flips back, under what condition, with which approval.
r5: Every kill event emits a structured log + alert: scope, actor, reason, timestamp; consumed by incident channel.
